SECTION 36(A). THE TRIAL DATE IS JULY 14, 2025, AT 11:15 A.M. IN DEPARTMENT 206. LAST DAY PURSUANT TO C.C.P. SECTION 36(F) IS JULY 23, 2025. THE LAST DAY FOR HEARING SUMMARY JUDGMENT/ADJUDICATION MOTIONS IS JULY 8, 2025. A FURTHER CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE IS SET FOR MAY 27, 2025, AT 4:00 P.M. A MANDATORY SETTLEMENT CONFERENCE IS SET FOR THURSDAY, JULY 10, 2025, AT 9:30 A.M. IN DEPARTMENT 503. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: ETHAN P. Off Calendar May-18-2026 Off Calendar- Case Settled At Settlement Confer.

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE - TRIAL COURTS
Off Calendar Apr-24-2026 Off Calendar

ASBESTOS ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Feb-24-2026 Set For Trial On Jun-15-26 At 11:15 A.M. In Dept 206

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Granting Preference In Setting Case For Trial, And Extending Discovery Cutoff
Off Calendar Feb-24-2026 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: No Appearances, Not contested, The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows:

Plaintiff's Motion for Order Granting Preference in Setting Case for Trial, and Extending Discovery Cutoff is GRANTED under C.C.P. sec 36(a). No opposition filed.

1. The trial date is June 15, 2026, at 11:15 a.m. in Department 206 is maintained.
a. Last day pursuant to C.C.P. section 36(f) is June 24, 2026.
b. The parties shall follow the California Rules of Court, San Francisco Local Rules, and Local Rule 20.

2. The last day for hearing summary judgment/adjudication motions is June 9, 2026.
a. Summary judgment/adjudication motions shall be brought on regular notice pursuant to the relevant provisions of the C.C.P., unless the parties stipulate otherwise.
b. Before a party files and serves a summary judgment/adjudication motion, it must contact the clerk to make a reservation.
c. The Court allows a maximum of four summary judgment/adjudication motions per day to be calendared, unless good cause is found to exceed this number. Contact the clerk to schedule a good cause hearing.

3. Time to respond to written discovery not yet served is shortened to 20 days.
a. For written discovery that has already been served, responses are due within 20 days of this hearing or by the date determined by the C.C.P., whichever is earlier.
b. Any issue/dispute that requires meet and confer, shall occur in person or via telephone, not by email or letter.

4. Electronic service is considered the equivalent of personal service.
5. The fact discovery cut-off date is May 29, 2026.
6. The expert discovery cut-off date is June 12, 2026.

7. All bankruptcy documents shall be turned over to defendants no later than March 26, 2026.
a. If Plaintiff submits documents to bankruptcy trusts after this date, they must notify Defendants no later than five days after submission.
b. The fact discovery cut-off does not apply to bankruptcy documents.

(Part 1 of 2, mini minute continues in part 2 of 2)

PLAINTIFF JOSEPH OCACIO'S MOTION FOR ORDER GRANTING PREFERENCE IN SETTING CASE FOR TRIAL, AND EXTENDING DISCOVERY CUTOFF
Off Calendar Feb-24-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: (Part 2 of 2, mini minute continues from previous entry)

8. No later than March 3, 2026:
a. Plaintiff must provide a statement to each Defendant stating the name and contact information of each lay person with knowledge of any facts supporting Plaintiff's claims as to each Defendant.
b. Defendants must provide a statement stating the name and contact information of each lay person with knowledge of facts supporting Defendant's affirmative defenses.
c. Any person not identified on the statements may be barred from testifying at trial or presenting a declaration on a summary judgment motion.

9. A further case management conference is set for April 28, 2026, at 4:00 p.m.
a. Case management conference statements are not required.
b. Parties may appear at the conference by zoom.
c. If the parties agree that no CMC is necessary, they shall notify the Court at least two court days before the hearing.

10. Plaintiffs will send a settlement demand to Defendants no later than May 4, 2026. Response to the settlement demand is due May 26, 2026. The parties shall separately submit confidential settlement demands/offers. This confidential value must be separately submitted to Department305@sftc.org via electronic mail on May 11, 2026.

11. A mandatory settlement conference is set for Thursday, June 11, 2026, at 9:30 a.m. in Department 305. Personal appearance is required by all unless a party is excused for good cause. A request to excuse a personal appearance must be submitted to Department305@sftc.org two weeks before the mandatory settlement conference.

The court to review the proposed order submitted by plaintiff.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Ed Santos and Jackie Alameda; Matter not reported; Bailiff: Not Present. (D304/EPS)

Off Calendar Mar-26-2025 Set For Trial On Jul-14-25 At 11:15 A.M. In Dept 206

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE - TRIAL COURTS
Off Calendar May-22-2025 Off Calendar

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Granting Preference In Setting Case For Trial, And Extending Discovery Cutoff
Off Calendar Mar-25-2025 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: No appearances; Not Contested; The Court adopts its tentative ruling:

Plaintiff's Motion for Order Granting Preference in Setting Case for Trial, and Extending Discovery Cutoff is GRANTED under C.C.P. section 36(a). Opposition filed by the Goodyear Tire & Rubber Company. Reply filed.

Defendant's opposition and conditional demands are meritless. "Where a party meets the requisite standard for calendar preference under [Code of Civil Procedure section 36] subdivision (a), preference must be granted. No weighing of interests is involved." (Fox v. Superior Court (2018) 21 Cal.App.5th 529, 535; see also, e.g., Miller v. Superior Court (1990) 221 Cal.App.3d 1200, 1204 [statute "grants a mandatory and absolute right to trial preference"]; Swaithes v. Superior Court (1989) 212 Cal.App.3d 1082, 1085 [trial court "has no power to balance the different interests of opposing litigants in applying the provision"]; Koch-Ash v. Superior Court (1986) 180 Cal.App.3d 689, 694 [section 36(a) "must be deemed to be mandatory and absolute" and "no discretion is left to trial courts."]
1. The trial date is July 14, 2025, at 11:15 a.m. in Department 206.
a. Last day pursuant to C.C.P. section 36(f) is July 23, 2025.
b. The parties shall follow the California Rules of Court, San Francisco Local Rules, and Local Rule 20.
2. The last day for hearing summary judgment/adjudication motions is July 8, 2025.
a. Summary judgment/adjudication motions shall be brought on regular notice pursuant to the relevant provisions of the C.C.P., unless the parties stipulate otherwise.
b. Before a party files and serves a summary judgment/adjudication motion, it must contact the clerk to make a reservation.
c. The Court allows a maximum of four summary judgment/adjudication motions per day to be calendared, unless good cause is found to exceed this number. Contact the clerk to schedule a good cause hearing.
3. Time to respond to written discovery not yet served is shortened to 20 days.
a. For written discovery that has already been served, responses are due within 20 days of this hearing or by the date determined by the C.C.P., whichever is earlier.
b. Any issue/dispute that requires meet and confer, shall occur in person or via telephone, not by email or letter.
4. Electronic service is considered the equivalent of personal service.
5. The fact discovery cut-off date is June 27, 2025.
6. The expert discovery cut-off date is July 11, 2025.
7. All bankruptcy documents shall be turned over to defendants no later than April 24, 2025.
a. If Plaintiff submits documents to bankruptcy trusts after this date, they must notify Defendants no later than five days after submission.
b. The fact discovery cut-off does not apply to bankruptcy documents.
(mini-minutes continues, see next entry)

b. Defendants must provide a statement stating the name and contact information of each lay person with knowledge of facts supporting Defendant's affirmative defenses.
c. Any person not identified on the statements may be barred from testifying at trial or presenting a declaration on a summary judgment motion.
9. A further case management conference is set for May 27, 2025, at 4:00 p.m.
a. Case management conference statements are not required.
b. Parties may appear at the conference by zoom.
c. If the parties agree that no CMC is necessary, they shall notify the Court at least two court days before the hearing.
10. Plaintiffs will send a settlement demand to Defendants no later than June 2, 2025. Response to the settlement demand is due June 23, 2025. The parties shall separately submit confidential settlement demands/offers. This confidential value must be separately submitted to Department503@sftc.org via electronic mail on June 30, 2025.

A request to excuse a personal appearance must be submitted to Department503@sftc.org two weeks before the mandatory settlement conference. Order signed.
